title: × leucantanacetum rohlenae
intro: × Leucantanacetum rohlenae is a rare and endangered hybrid plant, resulting from the cross between Leucanthemum vulgare and Tanacetum rohlenae, two separate plant species. It is believed to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant are thought to include relief from pain, reduced inflammation, and potential antimicrobial properties, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of various health conditions. However, due to its rarity and lack of extensive research, herbal preparations of × Leucantanacetum rohlenae are not commonly available, and its use is largely limited to traditional medicine and experimental studies.
